CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Continued To Feb-17-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SALESFORCE.COM, INC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff shall file an amended complaint within 20 days with leave to allege the date on which Plaintiff filed an administrative complaint with the Department of Fair Employment and Housing ("DFEH"). Defendant Salesforce.com, Inc.'s Demurrer to Complaint is overruled on all other grounds. Defendant Salesforce.com, Inc. contends that Plaintiff's first, second, third, and fourth causes of action are barred by the statute of limitations as Plaintiff did not file an administrative complaint with the DFEH within the one-year statute of limitations period. (Gov't Code section 12960(d).) Although Salesforce requested that the Court take judicial notice of two letters to Plaintiff from the DFEH, the substance of the DFEH letters are not subject to judicial notice. (Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 113 ["When judicial notice is taken of a document, however, the truthfulness and proper interpretation of the document are disputable."].) Additionally, the DFEH letters do not provide a filing date or a copy of Plaintiff's verified complaint. (RFJN, Exs. 4-5.) As a result, the proper interpretation of the letters is subject to reasonable dispute because the DFEH has the authority to file an unverified complaint before the statute of limitations runs. (Cal. Code Regs., Title 2 section 100008(b) ["The department may file a complaint for investigation without first obtaining the complainant's verification of the complaint, when doing so is necessary to avoid missing the statute of limitations for filing with the department."].) However, Plaintiff's general allegation that he timely filed a complaint with the DFEH (Compl., 16) is insufficient to determine whether his causes of action are time-barred. (Tenet Healthsystem Desert, Inc. v. Blue Cross of California (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 821, 834 ["a demurrer challenges defects on the face of the complaint."]; see Acuna v. San Diego Gas & Electric Co. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1402, 1413-1416 [finding that the plaintiff's factual allegations were detailed enough to show that the DFEH complaints were untimely].) Accordingly, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege the date of his administrative complaint.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: proceedings not reported.

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
